section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact schema_id is 'orch.tier5.excellence_section.v1', which is a Tier 2A-derived application-form schema, not a Grant Agreement Annex schema. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] references docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json and docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/evaluator_expectation_registry.json — both are application-form structural authorities. No strings matching 'Annex [0-9]', 'Grant Agreement Annex', 'AGA Template', or 'Model Grant Agreement template' appear as structural authorities in the artifact content or traceability footer.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Sub-section B.1.1 asserts specific call scope and expected-outcome claims: 'MAESTRO... directly addresses topic HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02, which calls for research on next-generation AI agents with advances in planning, reasoning, memory management, multi-agent coordination, and external tool and API integration. MAESTRO addresses all four scope research areas — advancing AI agent autonomy through structured planning and self-optimisation; innovation in memory-augmented AI for robust long-term reasoning; advanced multi-agent frameworks for collaborative agent coordination; and principled integration of external tools and APIs' and 'directly responsive to the call's two expected outcomes: significant improvements in AI agent autonomy, robustness, and reliability through planning, memory, and reasoning; and innovative multi-agent frameworks demonstrating effective decentralised coordination beyond individual agent capabilities.' The artifact's traceability_footer.primary_sources[] contains no reference to any path within docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/. The Tier 4 phase output proxies present (docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase1_call_analysis/call_analysis_summary.json and docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase2_concept_refinement/concept_refinement_summary.json) are downstream derivatives of Tier 2B but are not themselves Tier 2B extracted source references and do not satisfy the traceability requirement for a direct Tier 2B extracted source path.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, or equipment — not present in Tier 3
section13_checks.check_reasoning: All project-specific facts in the artifact — partner names (ATU, BIIS, CERIA, NIHS, ISIA, ELI, BAL, FIIT), capabilities (e.g., NIHS de-identified clinical data repository 3M+ patient records; ISIA sensor-instrumented Industry 4.0 pilot factory; BAL EuroHPC access; FIIT EDIH network six EU member states), objectives with measurable targets (OBJ-1 through OBJ-8), WP task identifiers, and deliverable due-months — all carry Tier 3 source references in traceability_footer.primary_sources[]: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json, docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/capabilities.json, docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json, docs/tier3_project_instantiation/project_brief/concept_note.md, docs/tier3_project_instantiation/project_brief/strategic_positioning.md, and docs/tier3_project_instantiation/project_brief/project_summary.json. Claim statuses C01–C14 are confirmed or inferred with Tier 3 source references. No project-fact claim is unresolved without a Tier 3 source reference.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Commencing any Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
section13_checks.check_reasoning: Content sub-check (budget figures in section text): A full scan of both sub_sections (B.1.1 and B.1.2) finds no patterns matching '€[0-9]+', '[0-9]+ EUR', or '[0-9]+ person-months' used as confirmed budget values. All numeric values are performance targets (percentages, agent counts, benchmark scores, operational periods in months) — not budget figures. Budget gate file sub-check: the budget_gate_assessment.json at docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/ is outside TAPM declared input boundaries (the input boundary rules prohibit reading docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/ in TAPM mode); gate_pass_declaration status cannot be verified from declared inputs. The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] does reference the budget gate assessment file as a cited source (entry with source_path: 'docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json'), indicating the artifact's authoring process was aware of this gate artifact. No budget-dependent claims are present in the section content itself.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
section13_checks.check_reasoning: Not directly verifiable from artifact content alone. This prohibition governs agent runtime behaviour, not artifact content. Structurally non-applicable to artifact-level audit. Enforcement is the responsibility of the decision-log-update skill and the orchestration runtime. Recorded as pass per skill specification §2 Check 13.5 handling.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
section13_checks.check_reasoning: Not directly verifiable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition governs workflow and skill scope boundary behaviour at runtime, not deliverable content. Structurally non-applicable to artifact-level audit. Recorded as pass per skill specification §2 Check 13.6 handling.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact's traceability_footer.primary_sources[] references Tier 4 phase outputs for phase1, phase2, phase3, and phase7 — all upstream of Phase 8 drafting. No reference to Phase 5 (impact_architecture) or Phase 6 (implementation_architecture) outputs appears, which is appropriate for an Excellence section. There is no indication that any higher-numbered phase output has been used as a prerequisite-substituting input for this section. The phase reference pattern is consistent with correct upstream sequencing for the Excellence criterion. No silent reordering is detectable.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact's validation_status.overall_status is 'unresolved' due to claim C15 (gender dimension in research content). However, the unresolved status IS explicitly and prominently flagged in the section content itself. Sub-section B.1.2 contains: 'Gender Dimension in Research and Innovation Content: The MAESTRO concept brief does not explicitly address how sex or gender analysis is incorporated into the research and innovation content. This is a gap requiring resolution before final proposal submission.' and 'This gap is declared unresolved and must be addressed before the proposal is finalised.' Additionally, the SSH assessment gap is flagged: 'A more detailed assessment of SSH relevance... should be developed for the full proposal.' The unresolved state is explicitly declared in the deliverable text — not papered over — satisfying the requirement of §13.8.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The artifact content does not assert Horizon Europe legal or regulatory claims (participation conditions, financial regulation terms, grant agreement legal provisions) of the type that would require Tier 1 source backing. References to EU programme infrastructure (AI-on-demand platform, European Digital Innovation Hubs, Testing and Experiment Facilities, AI Factories, Data Labs, EuroHPC) are organisational and programmatic references, not assertions about legal eligibility rules, participation conditions, or financial regulation provisions. The traceability_footer contains no Tier 1 references, but no claims of the type requiring Tier 1 backing (CLAUDE.md §13.9 — regulatory rules, participation conditions, grant agreement terms) are present in the content. No violation is detectable.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
section13_checks.check_reasoning: The traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration is false, triggering examination of claim_statuses[] for unresolved claims without source_refs. Only two non-confirmed claims exist: C12 (status: 'inferred', with Tier 3 and Tier 4 source references — inference chain stated) and C15 (status: 'unresolved', with source_ref: 'Tier 3 — docs/tier3_project_instantiation/project_brief/concept_note.md and docs/tier3_project_instantiation/project_brief/strategic_positioning.md...' explaining the gap). C15 has a source_ref even though the source_ref describes the absence of data rather than its presence — it points to Tier 3 documents and explains the gap. No unresolved claim exists without a source_ref in the traceability_footer. The no_unsupported_claims_declaration being false is consistent with the declared unresolved state of C15 and the inferred status of C12; these are appropriately disclosed, not silently asserted. No violation is detectable.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
section13_checks.check_reasoning: Not verifiable from a Tier 5 deliverable artifact. This prohibition governs write actions to Tier 1 and Tier 2 source documents, which cannot be detected by reading a Tier 5 deliverable. Structurally non-applicable to artifact-level audit. Recorded as pass per skill specification §2 Check 13.11 handling.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional
section13_checks.check_reasoning: Not directly verifiable from artifact content. This prohibition governs agent and process behaviour with respect to the constitution's binding status, not the content of a deliverable artifact. Structurally non-applicable to artifact-level audit. Recorded as pass per skill specification §2 Check 13.12 handling.

decision_log_entries.resolution_note: Constitutional violation detected; requires human operator resolution. The excellence_section.json asserts call-specific scope requirements and expected outcomes for HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02 without a direct Tier 2B extracted source reference in traceability_footer.primary_sources[]. The human operator must either (a) add a direct docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/ source reference to the artifact's traceability_footer.primary_sources[] and confirm the asserted call constraints and expected outcomes are faithfully reproduced from that source, or (b) if the Tier 2B extracted files are not yet populated, halt further use of this artifact until Phase 1 gate conditions are confirmed and a direct Tier 2B path reference is added to the traceability footer.
